tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment. Alkaline phosphatase is mildly elevated, and urine is abnormal in appearance, color, and content (protein, glucose, ketones), with low specific gravity. These findings are consistent with possible acute or chronic kidney disease, possibly with nephrotic features and metabolic derangements. Further nephrological evaluation is strongly recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
